title: The Sky Goes Dark in Daytime
story: Darkness fell in the middle of the day across North America in 2017. On August 21, millions of people gazed up in wonder as the moon completely blocked the sun, creating a total solar eclipse. For a few magical minutes, the sky turned dark, stars appeared, and a glowing ring of light surrounded the moon's silhouette. It was the first coast-to-coast total solar eclipse visible from the United States in nearly a century.
activity_question: What celestial event caused darkness to fall in the middle of the day?
follow_up_question: Why do you think total solar eclipses are so rare?

title: Voyager 2's Icy Neptune Adventure
story: Zooming through space, Voyager 2 reached Neptune on August 21, 1989. This plucky spacecraft became our eyes, peering at the distant blue planet. It snapped pictures of swirling storms, icy moons, and even discovered Neptune's speedy winds. Voyager 2's visit taught us so much about this far-off world, showing us its beautiful blue color and mysterious dark spot. The brave little probe continues its journey, carrying Earth's message to the stars.

title: The Mysterious Disappearance of Mona Lisa

title: Shaking Up Canada's West Coast
story: The ground rumbled and shook as Canada's most powerful earthquake in centuries struck in 1949. On August 21, the massive 8.1 magnitude quake rocked Haida Gwaii, an island chain off British Columbia's coast. The tremors were so strong that cows toppled over! title: Ballet Star Leaps to Freedom
story: A daring escape unfolded in New York City as Russian ballet star Alexander Godunov pirouetted away from his homeland. In 1979, on August 21, the Bolshoi Ballet dancer made the bold decision to defect, choosing freedom in America. title: Brave Stand for Library Equality

title: Vienna's Green Oasis Blooms
story: Flowers burst into bloom as Vienna's first public park opened its gates. The Vienna Stadtpark welcomed visitors on August 21, 1862, offering a lush green escape in the heart of the bustling city. Families strolled along winding paths, children played on grassy lawns, and musicians filled the air with melodies. This beautiful oasis quickly became a favorite spot for picnics and relaxation.
activity_question: What opened its gates in Vienna in 1862?
bonus_fact: The Stadtpark is home to a famous golden statue of composer Johann Strauss II.

title: Clash of Fleets: De Ruyter's Victory
story: Cannons boomed and sails billowed as Dutch Admiral Michiel de Ruyter led his fleet into battle on August 21, 1673. At Kijkduin, off the Dutch coast, he faced a powerful combined English and French navy. Despite being outnumbered, De Ruyter's clever tactics and brave sailors won the day. This victory helped protect the Netherlands and showed De Ruyter's skill as a naval commander.
activity_question: Who led the Dutch fleet to victory in this sea battle?
bonus_fact: Michiel de Ruyter is considered one of the greatest admirals in naval history.

title: Looney Tunes Creator Draws First Breath
story: Pencils and paintbrushes danced with joy as Friz Freleng entered the world. Born in Kansas City, Missouri on August 21, 1905, Freleng would grow up to become a legendary animator. His creative genius brought life to beloved Warner Bros cartoons, including the wacky characters of Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies that still make us laugh today.
activity_question: What famous cartoon series did Friz Freleng help create?
follow_up_question: Can you name any Looney Tunes characters?

title: Punk Rock Pioneer Born in Turkey
story: Joe Strummer, the future voice of punk rock, entered the world in Ankara, Turkey in 1952. Born on August 21, he would grow up to become the lead singer and songwriter for The Clash. Strummer's powerful vocals and rebellious spirit helped shape iconic songs like 'Rock the Casbah' and 'London Calling', influencing generations of musicians.
activity_question: Where was Joe Strummer born?
bonus_fact: The Clash's music often addressed social and political issues.

title: A Musical Prodigy's Remarkable Journey
story: Lili Boulanger's musical talent bloomed early. Born in Paris in 1893, she astounded listeners with perfect pitch at age two. By her teens, Lili was composing breathtaking works. On August 21, she made history as the first woman to win the prestigious Prix de Rome prize for music composition. Her victory opened doors for future female composers.
activity_question: What special ability did Lili Boulanger demonstrate at age two?
bonus_fact: Lili Boulanger's career was tragically short. She passed away at just 24 years old.
